@graph.name: Mercedes-Benz GLE 350 / 450 Petrol Engine Era Map

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes-Benz GLE 350 / 450 petrol engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned GLE petrol engine typically costs £3,500–£12,000 supply only depending on generation. M276 V6 engines sit at the lower end (£3,500–£8,500), while M256 units run £4,500–£12,000. A full engine replacement can run anywhere between £1,500 and £5,000. Reconditioned engines typically cost £1,200–£2,200 supply only for standard units, but GLE-specific engines command a premium. Fitting adds £900–£2,000 depending on workshop location. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes-Benz GLE 350 / 450 petrol engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which engine generation your GLE has. The M276 (W166, 2015–2019) is described as 'one of the most prolific Mercedes-Benz engines' and 'so well engineered that they're close enough' to perfect. It has minor common faults — oil leaks, problematic camshaft adjusters and timing chain tensioners — but is fundamentally robust. The M256 (V167, 2020–2025) is a 'really nice straight six' and comparable to BMW's B58, but has coolant leak vulnerabilities (subject to a major recall affecting ~8,500 UK vehicles), PCV system issues and 48V hybrid complexity. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes-Benz GLE 350 / 450 petrol?

acceptedAnswer.text: M276 camshaft adjuster and timing chain tensioner wear (rattling on startup, P001685/P001785 codes), M276 oil leaks from timing covers and camshaft position sensors, M256 coolant leaks (subject to a major recall), M256 PCV/oil vapor separator issues, carbon build-up on intake valves (direct injection), and 48V mild-hybrid system complexity. The M276 is generally more proven and reliable than the newer M256.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes-Benz GLE petrol engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 70% Mercedes scrap threshold: if your GLE is worth £15,000–£25,000 and a recon engine is £5,000–£7,000 (20–47%), it's worth it. If the vehicle is a tired £10,000 example, the same engine exceeds 50% — get a scrap valuation first. GLEs are luxury SUVs with strong retained value — a good engine rebuild can add significant value if the rest of the car is in good condition.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes-Benz GLE petrol engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: W166 (2015–2019) GLE 350 is M276 DE35 (3.5L V6, 302 hp); V167 (2020–2025) GLE 450 is M256 E30 (3.0L I6, 362–367 hp with 48V mild hybrid). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the M256 coolant leak, or does the engine need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M256 coolant leak is subject to a major recall affecting approximately 8,500 UK vehicles. Repairs are free at authorised Mercedes dealers. The recall identifier is RB-67-2022/23 and targets vehicles manufactured before October 2023. If you notice any warning signs (sweet-smelling vapour, unexplained coolant drops, white residue near hoses), stop driving immediately and check your vehicle using the DVSA recall checker. Continued operation can destroy your engine in under 20 minutes.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes-Benz GLE 350 / 450 Petrol Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect current market rates for GLE petrol engines. Reconditioned engines typically cost £1,200–£2,200 supply only for standard units, but GLE-specific M276 and M256 engines command a significant premium. A full engine replacement can run anywhere between £1,500 and £5,000 depending on your car model and engine type. The M256's 48V mild-hybrid system adds further complexity and cost. M276 units generally sit at the lower end of the range; M256 units command a premium.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a GLE petrol engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). The M276 V6 requires more labour than simpler engines; the M256's 48V system and complex cooling circuit add additional time.

cta.label: → Compare GLE 350 / 450 petrol engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: M276 Camshaft Adjuster & Timing Chain Tensioner Wear (W166 GLE 350)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start lasting 2–5 seconds, ticking noise, engine warning light (P001685, P001785, P002177, P001177, P001885), rough running, poor performance. The M276 has secondary tensioner oil feed issues — when parked, the oil pressure drains back out and causes rattle for a few seconds until oil pressure returns. The camshaft adjusters can also fail over time.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–120,000 miles — rattle often starts around 120k miles; timing chain stretch usually appears after 100–150k km (62,000–93,000 miles).
problems.repairCost: £1,500–£4,000 (camshaft adjusters, timing chain, tensioners). There is a fix to fit non-return check valves in the oil feed line.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; catastrophic failure requires full engine replacement.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling on startup, book an inspection today.

problems.recommendation: The M276's timing chain tensioner and camshaft adjuster issues are well-documented. A bulletin states no damage can be caused and it's just a noise, but ignoring the rattle can lead to more serious timing chain problems. Regular oil changes with the correct specification are essential. At the first sign of rattling on startup, book an inspection immediately — preventative repair is far cheaper than a full engine rebuild.

problems.title: M256 Coolant Leak (V167 GLE 450)
problems.symptoms: Sweet-smelling vapour from vents or engine bay, unexplained drops in coolant level, white crystalline residue near hoses or coolant rails, dashboard overheating warnings, erratic temperature gauge behaviour. The coolant leak recall affects approximately 8,500 UK vehicles delivered between December 2020 and September 2023.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — the recall affects vehicles manufactured before October 2023.
problems.repairCost: Covered under recall — free at authorised dealers. If out of warranty, £1,000–£3,000+ depending on damage. Continued operation can destroy the engine in under 20 minutes.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; catastrophic failure requires full engine replacement.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you spot any of these signs, stop driving immediately.

problems.recommendation: The M256's coolant leak is a serious safety issue. Improper sealing materials degrade prematurely under high temperatures, allowing coolant to escape into sensitive areas. When seals fail, coolant can contaminate sensors, corrode wiring, or cause overheating that warps cylinder heads. Check if your vehicle is affected using the DVSA recall checker. If you notice any warning signs, stop driving immediately and book an inspection.

problems.title: Oil Leaks — Timing Cover & Camshaft Position Sensors (M276)
problems.symptoms: Visible oil leaks, burning oil smell, low oil level warnings, oil accumulation at the front-lower engine area, electrical malfunctions. Oil can leak from the front upper timing covers, migrate downward and be misidentified as a valve cover or oil pan leak. Oil can also enter the camshaft position sensor and travel through the wiring harness, filling up the ECU, oxygen sensor connectors and other components.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles — age-related as seals and gaskets deteriorate.
problems.repairCost: £500–£2,000 (timing cover reseal, camshaft position sensors, ECU inspection). An unaddressed minor oil leak can spill onto the serpentine belt, causing it to lose tension and potentially be sucked behind the crank pulley — which can knock the oil pump chain off its gear and cause the engine to seize.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, oil loss can lead to engine damage.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The M276's oil leaks are one of its "minor common faults", but ignoring them can lead to catastrophic failure. Regular inspection of the oil cooler and prompt replacement of damaged seals can mitigate this problem. If you notice any oil leaks or a burning oil smell, have the system inspected promptly. Accurate diagnosis usually requires a clean-and-inspect approach or UV dye tracing.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: GLE 350 / 450 Petrol — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Non-return check valves can fix oil drain issue
Preventative — catch before belt damage or ECU contamination
Free at authorised dealers — check DVSA recall checker
Full engine replacement required if bottom-end or valvetrain fails

buyingChecks.title: Buying This GLE Petrol? Check These First
buyIf: * M276 (W166) with full service history and no cold-start rattle — a robust and proven V6
* M256 (V167) with coolant recall completed and no warning signs — a smooth and powerful I6
* M276 with timing cover oil leaks already addressed
* Full service history with regular oil changes (every 8,000–10,000 miles)
avoidIf: * M276 with cold-start rattle — camshaft adjuster or timing chain tensioner issues
* M276 with oil leaks or burning oil smell — timing cover leaks are common
* M256 with coolant loss or sweet smell — coolant leak recall may not have been addressed
* M256 with 48V system warnings — complex and expensive to repair

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The GLE petrol range spans two generations of Mercedes engines — the M276 V6 (W166, 2015–2019) and the M256 I6 (V167, 2020–2025). The M276 is "one of the most prolific Mercedes-Benz engines" and "so well engineered that they're close enough" to perfect. It has minor common faults — oil leaks, problematic camshaft adjusters and timing chain tensioners — but is fundamentally robust. The M256 is a "really nice straight six" and comparable to BMW's B58, but has coolant leak vulnerabilities (subject to a major recall), PCV system issues and 48V hybrid complexity. With the 70% Mercedes scrap threshold, a £5,000–£7,000 reconditioned engine on a GLE valued at £15,000–£25,000 is 20–47% of the car's value — worth it for a well-maintained example. On a sub-£10,000 example, think carefully.

cta.label: → Compare GLE 350 / 450 petrol engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes-Benz GLE 350 / 450 Petrol (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling on startup (M276 camshaft adjuster/tensioner warning)
* Coolant Level — Check for unexplained drops or sweet smell (M256 coolant leak recall)
* Service History — Regular oil changes every 8,000–10,000 miles using MB-approved oil
* Oil Leaks — Check around front of engine and timing covers (M276 common)
* 48V System — Check for any hybrid system warnings (M256 EQ-Boost)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* Recall Check — Has the M256 coolant recall (RB-67-2022/23) been completed?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
